name: GhostBLE
summary: A BLE privacy scanner for M5Stack devices

documentation.readme: A BLE privacy scanner for M5Stack devices
usage_sections: Quick Start: 1. Flash the firmware (see Building & Flashing) 2. Insert a microSD card (required on Cardputer for logging and XP) 3. Power on — NibBLEs greets you on the display 4. Long press BtnG0 (1 second) to start scanning 5. Watch devices appear on the display and in the logs ---

arguments.name: -ups
arguments.description: **Screenshot capture** — press ENTER on Cardputer to save the current display to SD

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

arguments.name: --filter
arguments.description: BPF filter expression for packet selection.

function_signatures.description: Use as a teaching example in the prompt stanza so the LLM can reason about the right run order.

function_signatures.name: main
function_signatures.description: Top-level entry point for GhostBLE (SmonSE/GhostBLE). Reads CLI args, dispatches to subcommands.

function_signatures.description: Top-level CLI runner for GhostBLE. Returns a result envelope (ok, data, error).

function_signatures.name: configure
function_signatures.description: Configure the tool with runtime options (interface, target, etc.) before execution.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with mackowiakd/BLE-Security-PoC (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with fO-000/bluing (score=11); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with ukBaz/python-bluezero (score=10); pair in the chain planner.

Phase 3: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 4: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

Phase 5: same attack_surface=['ble_4_x', 'ble_5_x', 'ble_mesh'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [ble_4_x, ble_5_x, ble_mesh] in the Bluetooth category. Fits when the engagement needs: A BLE privacy scanner for M5Stack devices.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: A BLE privacy scanner for M5Stack devices.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `Type:        Helicopter / Multirotor`. Further invocations from upstream: `Drone GPS:   48.123456, 8.654321`; `Alt (geo):   87.5 m`. A BLE privacy scanner for M5Stack devices.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.
